tweets.text:
Global oil inventories can fall to 1 billion bbls.

No it can’t. 

Here’s a very simple illustration how this is physically impossible. 

US commercial crude storage, the most visible storage in the world, is 5.5% of global oil inventories, 443 million bbls out of 8 billion bbls. 

US commercial crude storage is the storage of last resort. You can think of it as a balancing mechanism for the oil market. Naturally US commercial crude storage builds. US has to export all the excess light sweet crude produced by US shale. So if there’s no demand for it, exports drop. If there is demand for it, export jumps, inventory declines. 

In theory, US commercial crude storage’s relative percentage to global oil inventories will be between 4.5-5%. 

Assuming 1 billion bbls globally, 4.5% of that is 45 million bbls. That’s stupid as fuck. 

There’s 135 million bbls in linefill alone, oil needed to keep pipelines operational. 

That’s why I’ve never spent anytime addressing this idiotic statement figuring that the rest of the market will see it for what it is: pure asinine stupidity. 

It is what it is.

tweets.text:
Memorial Day weekend fake MOU hype will be remembered in history. 

Within hours, within days, Trump’s options and time are running out. 

By the end of June, if the Strait of Hormuz is still closed, global oil inventory operational minimum is guaranteed. China is next. Watch China’s entire onshore inventory drain to nothing if it doesn’t return to the market. 

Time is ticking. Tick tock, tick tock. 

⏳
